title: glyceria arundinacea subsp. grandis
intro: Glyceria arundinacea subsp. grandis, also known as tall mannagrass, is a perennial grass native to Europe and Asia. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been shown to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving digestive health, due to its saponins and flavonoids content. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Glyceria arundinacea subsp. grandis can be dried and used as a tea or made into a decoction to harness its medicinal properties.
